subject: Sports
title: Larisa Latynina's Record Haul
description: A micro-lesson that makes Larisa Latynina's Record Haul usable.

hook.text: Before Michael Phelps, one woman held the record for most Olympic medals—for nearly 50 years.

buildup.text: Soviet gymnast Larisa Latynina competed at three Olympics: 1956, 1960, and 1964.

discovery.text: She won 18 medals—nine gold, five silver, four bronze. No one matched her total until Phelps in 2012.

twist.text: She competed at the 1958 World Championships while five months pregnant—and still won gold.

climax.text: Latynina later coached the Soviet Olympic gymnastics team, passing her excellence to the next generation.

punchline.text: Her 18-medal record stood for 48 years. She earned every one.

quiz.question: How many Olympic medals did Larisa Latynina win?
